rationale: The cited paper appears once, in a grouped citation with Montgomery et al. (2004), to support the claim that clickstream browsing metrics can indicate engagement and purchase likelihood. In the citing paper, this serves as brief background justification for using pages browsed as an outcome in the field experiment, not as a direct adoption of Bucklin and Sismeiro's specific model or estimation approach. The main theory and contribution on hassle costs and period-level bracketing do not otherwise build on this cited work in a sustained way.
